parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces a critical new retry/backoff subsystem (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) and deletes process.mjs, but ships without corresponding v0.4.6 UAT evidence — only v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 have test-automation reports. The highest-risk code (auto-retry on BigModel errors) has no end-to-end validation in the repo. Combined with a deleted module and a large un-vetted surface, this should not ship without verifying retry safety and import completeness.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 retry/backoff feature
findings.body: The highest-risk change in this diff is the BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff system (commit e0a9ba8, v0.4.6). The repo contains test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.4/ and v0.4.5/ with multi-scenario runbooks, but there is no test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ directory. The retry module (scripts/lib/retry.mjs, 203 new lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 new lines) govern API call resilience — exactly the kind of code that fails under rate-limiting, partial responses, and transient errors that only appear in live runs. Unit tests (tests/retry.test.mjs, tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs) exist but are synthetic; they cannot validate actual backoff timing against a real BigModel endpoint or confirm that retried requests are idempotent at the API level.
findings.recommendation: Run and record a v0.4.6 UAT cycle that exercises at minimum: (1) a request that triggers a retryable BigModel error, (2) backoff exhaustion, (3) a non-retryable error, and (4) a successful retry. Archive the report alongside the existing v0.4.4/v0.4.5 evidence before tagging release.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — latent import crash risk
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was completely removed in this diff. If any remaining file — a command, a hook, a CI script, or a dynamic import resolved at runtime — still references 'scripts/lib/process.mjs' or './process.mjs', it will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime with no prior warning. The check-imports.mjs script (5 lines changed) may validate static imports but cannot catch dynamic import() calls or string-interpolated paths. This is the kind of failure that passes CI but breaks in production when an uncommon code path is exercised.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any reference to 'process.mjs' (including dynamic import() expressions, string literals, and test files). Confirm check-imports.mjs covers dynamic imports. If any reference exists, either restore the module with a re-export shim or update the call site.

findings.title: Retry module may lack idempotency protection for non-idempotent BigModel operations
findings.body: retry.mjs (203 new lines) provides generic retry with backoff for BigModel API calls. If any BigModel operation is non-idempotent (e.g., job submission, state mutation), blindly retrying on a timeout or 5xx could cause duplicate jobs or double state transitions. The diff stat shows no new idempotency-key or dedup mechanism. Without seeing the code, the concern is: a POST that reaches the server but whose response is lost would be retried, creating a duplicate. The tracked-jobs.mjs changes (78 lines) and state.mjs changes (32 lines) may or may not guard against this.
findings.recommendation: Audit retry.mjs to confirm it differentiates between idempotent (GET) and non-idempotent (POST/mutation) calls. For non-idempotent calls, either: (a) do not retry automatically, or (b) attach an idempotency key that the server can use to deduplicate. Document which BigModel operations are safe to retry.

findings.title: configure-gitea-protection.sh in repo could be accidentally executed against wrong host
findings.body: scripts/setup/configure-gitea-protection.sh (126 new lines) configures branch protection rules on a Gitea instance. If a developer runs this script with stale or wrong environment variables, it could silently apply incorrect protection rules or overwrite existing ones on a production Gitea server. The CI exclusion noted in commit 76bdbd7 ('exclude configure-gitea-protection.sh from path-leak guard') means it is intentionally present but the guard gap also means it won't be caught by the leak checker if it contains hardcoded hostnames.
findings.recommendation: Add a prominent safety check at the top of the script: require an explicit --target-host flag or environment variable, refuse to run against localhost or production unless an additional --i-understand-the-risk flag is passed, and print a dry-run summary before making changes.

next_steps: Grep entire codebase for any remaining reference to scripts/lib/process.mjs and confirm no runtime path can reach a deleted import.
Run and archive v0.4.6 UAT covering retry, backoff exhaustion, and error classification before promoting past v0.4.6.
Audit retry.mjs for idempotency safety on POST-like BigModel operations; add guards or document safe-to-retry operations.
Review configure-gitea-protection.sh for host-safety guards before any developer accidentally targets a production Gitea instance.
